query: What is MiFID II and why does it matter for our clients?
answer: MiFID II (Markets in Financial Instruments Directive II) is a European Union legislative framework that regulates financial markets and strengthens investor protection. It matters for our clients because it requires SIX Group client advisors to conduct suitability assessments, ensure transparency in pricing and execution, and classify financial products as complex or non-complex — directly affecting what we can recommend to each client type. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Directly supported by internal Legal documentation.

citations.author: Legal Team
citations.relevant_quote: MiFID II is a European Union legislative framework that regulates financial markets and strengthens investor protection.

query: What counts as a complex instrument under MiFID II?
answer: Under MiFID II, complex instruments include: derivatives (options, futures, swaps), structured products embedding derivatives or leverage, convertible/exchangeable bonds, callable and puttable bonds, and any instrument whose performance depends on indices, commodities or credit events. Non-complex instruments include ordinary shares, money market instruments, plain bonds, and UCITS funds. Client advisors must complete an appropriateness assessment before selling complex instruments to retail clients. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Definition explicitly listed in internal Legal documentation.

citations.author: Legal Team
citations.relevant_quote: Complex instruments under MiFID II include: derivatives such as options, futures, swaps...

query: How do I conduct a suitability assessment?
answer: A suitability assessment under MiFID II covers three dimensions: (1) Knowledge and Experience — the client's familiarity with the product, trading history, and education; (2) Financial Situation — income, assets, liabilities and ability to bear losses; (3) Investment Objectives — risk tolerance and purpose. A product is only suitable when it matches all three dimensions. You must document the rationale in a suitability report delivered to the client before the transaction. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Procedure fully described in the suitability assessment internal document.

citations.author: Legal Team
citations.relevant_quote: The suitability test covers three dimensions: Knowledge and Experience, Financial Situation, Investment Objectives.

query: How many days of annual leave do I get as a senior staff member?
answer: Senior staff at SIX Group (Grades 5–7) are entitled to 27 days of annual leave per year. Junior staff (Grades 1–4) receive 25 days, and management (Grades 8+) receive 30 days. Part-time employees have their entitlement prorated to contracted hours. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Entitlement figures directly stated in the HR Leave Policy document.

query: What is the performance rating scale at SIX?
answer: SIX Group uses a 5-point rating scale: 1 – Significantly Below Expectations, 2 – Below Expectations, 3 – Meets Expectations, 4 – Exceeds Expectations, 5 – Outstanding. Ratings of 1 or 2 trigger a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) with HR involvement. Ratings of 4 or 5 are considered in the year-end compensation review. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Rating scale explicitly defined in the Performance Review Process document.

answer: Good morning! I'm AskMe, SIX Group's internal knowledge assistant. How can I help you today? Feel free to ask me about our policies, compliance procedures, client onboarding, or any other topic covered in our internal documents.

query: What are the steps for onboarding a new client?
answer: The SIX Group client onboarding procedure has four steps: (1) KYC Verification — collect government-issued ID, proof of address, and tax ID; (2) AML Screening — screen against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists and PEP databases; (3) Risk Profiling — complete Form CS-01 to determine risk category (Conservative, Balanced, or Growth); (4) Account Activation — after compliance review (target 3 business days) the Relationship Manager activates the account in CORE. Any missing document or adverse screening result must be escalated to the Senior Compliance Officer within 2 hours. [REF-1]

confidence.reason: Full onboarding procedure documented step-by-step in the Customer Service policy.

citations.author: Customer Service Team
